published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024: generic sharp bounds for discrete causal settings subsume the computational LP idea but not the analytic full-history separation

repo_prior_art.cite: none in ThmSmith catalogue; static/dynamic exact-ID endpoints are outside the permitted repo-axis catalogue

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 for T=1 binary-IV bounds; standard sequential-randomization IPW for exact-compliance collapse

repo_prior_art.cite: closest cited boundary is ExactID/DynamicLATE, not an in-catalogue partial-ID no-separation theorem

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Duarte-style polynomial-program wording to Theorem 1's novelty block: call it a transparent specialization, not a new bounding paradigm.
For Conjecture 1, require the eventual certificate to report both full-history and compressed dual witnesses; this will make strictness auditable rather than numerical.
For Conjecture 3, define the finite enumeration invariant more operationally, including how objective coefficients are compared after canonicalization.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with a small rational T=3 LP certificate search for Conjectures 1 and 2, keeping Theorem 2 as a sanity endpoint only.
